title: Zanthoxylum machadoi
intro: Zanthoxylum machadoi, also known as Machado's prickly ash, is a plant species in the Rutaceae family, characterized by its thorny stems and aromatic foliage. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as pain, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Zanthoxylum machadoi include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as essential oils and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
